_comment: Source of truth for the standard package set installed THROUGH the language version managers (node->npm, python->pipx/uv), distinct from the managers themselves (those are vmgr's job). Includes the docker_wrapper tools whose non-docker fallback is a node/python install (prettier, markdownlint, yamllint). docs/packages.md is GENERATED from this file by bin/gen-package-doc; edit here, not there. The version managers themselves (uv, pipx) are intentionally NOT packages here - see the excluded list. SCOPE: only node/python-installable apps are evaluated so far - the docker_wrapper tools built on other toolchains (shellcheck/hadolint = Haskell; shfmt/trivy/dive = Go) still await per-language evaluation - see TODO 'Package Install Setup'. SERVICES (e.g. ollama, openwebui) are deliberately out of scope: they are managed separately, mostly on the Linode server, not this dev machine - see the excluded list.
_icebox: ICEBOX: ansible / ansible-core - consider adding ansible to this manifest (python, pipx/pip-installable). Already used for packer image builds in $PROJECTS_DIR/harleydev and $PROJECTS_DIR/ansible-stuff. Deferred (maybe-someday); revisit on request.
priority_note: Install methods are listed in priority order. Docker is preferred where a dedicated, sensible image exists (docker.viable); otherwise the language manager (npm for node, pipx then uv for python).
docker_viable_meaning: True when a dedicated/official image exists AND running the tool in a container makes sense for normal use; false when there is no dedicated image (run via a generic image or an aggregate linter), when containerizing the tool is awkward (it manages its own environments), or when it is pointless (a per-project dev dependency).

packages.name: aider-chat
packages.summary: AI pair-programming assistant in the terminal

docker.note: No dedicated official image; run via a generic python image or an aggregate linter (e.g. MegaLinter).
No dedicated official image; a bin/docker_wrapper entry is planned (see TODO 'Docker tooling Setup'), after which Docker becomes the preferred route.
Wrapped in this repo's bin/docker_wrapper (prettier image).

packages.name: commitizen
packages.summary: Conventional-commit prompt with changelog and version bumping

docker.note: No official image; it manages its own (often containerized) hook environments, so running pre-commit itself in Docker is awkward.
Already wrapped in this repo's bin/docker_wrapper (cytopia/yamllint).

packages.name: poetry
packages.summary: Python dependency management and packaging tool

docker.note: No canonical CLI-only image; usually layered into a python build image rather than run standalone.

packages.name: pytest
packages.note: Normally a per-project dev dependency inside the project's venv, not a global tool; a global install is only for ad-hoc use.

docker.note: Run via a python image in CI; not meaningful as a standalone global container.
Official image; downloads voice models on first use.

packages.name: taskwarrior-scalpel
packages.summary: First-party Taskwarrior AI-planning toolkit (many scalpel-* entry points)
packages.note: First-party tool; install from its own source repo, not a public package.

docker.note: First-party tool; not published as an image.

excluded.name: uv
excluded.reason: A language version manager itself - installed and updated by `vmgr install python uv` (lib/version-managers/python), not through this package manifest.

excluded.name: pipx
excluded.reason: A package-manager runtime, not a package - bootstrapped by `vmgr install python pipx` (lib/version-managers/python). It is the primary installer the python entries above use.

excluded.name: ollama
excluded.reason: A self-hosted AI model-runtime service, not a dev package - managed separately (most services run on the Linode server, not this dev machine) and Docker-native. Available locally via `bin/docker_wrapper`.

excluded.name: openwebui
excluded.reason: A self-hosted web-UI service (python-installable, but service-oriented), not a dev package - managed separately (most services run on the Linode server) and Docker-native. Available locally via `bin/docker_wrapper`.
